title: Curve, Vyper - REKT
relevance_summary: Vyper compiler bug leading to reentrancy attacks on Curve pools

prevention.rule: Block any tx that calls add_liquidity() or remove_liquidity() in the same tx if the contract is written in Vyper versions 0.2.15, 0.2.16, or 0.3.0.
prevention.trigger: add_liquidity() -> remove_liquidity() in the same tx for Vyper versions 0.2.15, 0.2.16, or 0.3.0

red_flags: reentrancy pattern within the same transaction
calls to add_liquidity() and remove_liquidity() in the same tx
monitoring_rules: Block any tx that calls add_liquidity() or remove_liquidity() in the same tx if the contract is written in Vyper versions 0.2.15, 0.2.16, or 0.3.0.
summary: A Vyper compiler bug allowed attackers to exploit reentrancy vulnerabilities in Curve pools, draining millions of dollars across multiple protocols. A guard could have blocked transactions calling both add_liquidity() and remove_liquidity() in the same tx for Vyper versions known to be vulnerable.
